reason_of_clustering: Gold and Silver are both classified as precious metals and often move in relation to one another in response to economic indicators, inflation expectations, and geopolitical tensions.
Gold is considered a safe-haven asset that often appreciates during times of economic uncertainty, whereas the Dollar Index can reflect the strength of the USD, influencing gold prices inversely.
Both the Dollar Index and Silver serve as indicators of market sentiment and economic health, with movements in the Dollar impacting the pricing of Silver in global markets.
